channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky explains how he helped Sam Altman during OpenAI’s 2023 board fiasco
item.description: After 17 years, Airbnb’s Brian Chesky is hitting reset—reinventing the business from the ground up and expanding the brand in unexpected ways.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tesla promises more affordable EVs and could start its Robotaxi service in June – but I've heard this all before
item.description: Elon Musk makes more big Tesla promises during a quarterly earnings call, but I'd like to see it go back to doing what it does best.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: President Trump to meet with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump is meeting Friday with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, whose company designs and supplies the advanced computer chips that play an integral role in developing artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Kevin Freking And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A new Chrome browser highjacking attack could affect billions of users - here's how to fight it
item.description: There's a new highjacking attack that could take over your Chrome browser and steal all your data - here's how to keep your computer safe.
item.dc:creator: allisa.james@futurenet.com (Allisa James), TechRadar

item.title: Disability monitoring tool leaked personal information online
item.description: AngelSense was leaking names, location data, and more to anyone with a browser and knowledge of the right IP address.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 9 Google Chrome features you really should be using
item.description: If you’re like about 70% of computer users worldwide, you use Google’s Chrome browser as your gateway to the web, from conducting research and catching up on news to emailing and interacting with cloud apps. There are several tools built into Chrome that you might not know about, but should.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The quantum computing reality check
item.description: In late 2024, a major pharmaceutical company invested $50 million in quantum computing initiatives through a leading cloud provider, hoping to revolutionize their drug discovery process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Grim memories resurface after DNA advances lead to arrest of suspect in Hawaii teen's 1977 murder
item.description: The unsolved death of a teenager in 1977 haunted students and staff at Honolulu's McKinley High School for nearly half a century.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Coming soon
item.description: The Deepseek-R1 model has managed to attract a lot of attention in a short time, especially because it can be used commercially without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reid Hoffman says that AI can give you superpowers
item.description: Reid Hoffman returns to Rapid Response to explore today’s AI landscape, and the future promised by a concept he calls “superagency.” Hoffman shares his vision for what an AI-infused workday will soon look like, how we should address society’s greatest fears about technology, and more.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI to partner with US National Laboratories to boost national security
item.description: OpenAI announced Thursday it is partnering with the Los Alamos National Laboratory to install its newest artificial intelligence models onto the lab's supercomputer for national security research. "We care a lot about AI and science, we've talked about this for a very long time.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: NASA's two stuck astronauts are taking their first spacewalk together, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump World learns how to deal with Musk
item.description: President Trump’s allies and aides are learning to deal with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who has had a consistent presence in the early days of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Amazon secretly tracked Californian consumers via cellphones, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Amazon.com was sued on Wednesday by consumers who accused the retailing giant of secretly tracking their movements through their cellphones, and selling data it collects.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Don’t be so quick to click that Google Calendar invite. It could be a hacker’s trap
item.description: A phishing campaign uses Google Calendar to schedule fake meeting invitations that appear legitimate, redirecting targets to phishing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top 8 outages of 2024
item.description: Network outages continued to plague organizations of all sizes in 2024, with major incidents exposing vulnerabilities in digital infrastructure and the growing complexity of IT environments, according to ThousandEyes’ recent Internet outage report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Outsmart hackers who are out to steal your identity
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers tips on how to protect your identity: Be vigilant, monitor accounts, use smart security, know theft response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
